understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each level by moving the selectable piece(s) so that every goal tile marked '+' in the semantic grid was occupied by a selectable at the same time; completing a level required all goals covered simultaneously, and completing the run required winning through all levels within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement used MOVE_* actions that attempt to shift the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) per action, with x increasing to the right and y increasing downward. Walls/boundaries and adjacent objects could block or alter movement (a MOVE could have no effect if blocked). Some objects (blocks) were pushable if directly adjacent in the movement direction, moving together with the selected piece when pushed, while walls generally prevented passage. If multiple selectables existed, CLICK with an exact pixel-coordinate target_position (matching the positions listed in the state) was used to switch which selectable was controlled; incorrect coordinates or blocked selection resulted in no change.
